title: picris hieracioides subsp. umbellata
intro: Picris hieracioides subsp. umbellata, also known as Picris umbellata, is a plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae), characterized by its narrow leaves and small yellow flowers. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic effects, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Picris umbellata include the relief of urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a dried herb, tea, or tincture to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
